quality.rationale: 变分法推导场方程与能动张量，结合协变导数性质与运动方程验证守恒律，物理逻辑链条完整且具有标准GR场论结构

origin.question: Problem 21.5. The Lagrangian density for a scalar field is\n$\\mathbf{L} = -(8\\pi)^{-1}(\\Phi_{;a}\\Phi^{;a} + \\mathfrak{m}^{2}\\Phi^{2})$ . Find the equations of motion and the stress-energy tensor. Verify explicitly that the stress-energy tensor has vanishing divergence.
origin.answer: Equations of motion: $\\square \\Phi - m^2 \\Phi = 0$. Stress-energy tensor: $T_{\\mu\\nu} = \\frac{1}{4\\pi} \\left[ \\Phi_{,\\mu} \\Phi_{,\\nu} - \\frac{1}{2} g_{\\mu\\nu} (\\Phi_{,\\alpha} \\Phi^{,\\alpha} + m^2 \\Phi^2) \\right]$. Divergence: $T^{\\mu\\nu}_{;\\nu} = 0$.
origin.solution:
Solution 21.5. Taking the variation of the action
$$
S = - (8 \pi)^{- 1} \int (\Phi_{; a} \Phi^{; a} + m^{2} \Phi^{2}) (- g)^{\frac {1}{2}} d^{4} x
$$
givessus
$$
0 = \frac {\delta \mathbf {L}}{\delta \Phi} = \left[ 2 \mathfrak {m}^{2} \Phi - 2 (- \mathfrak {g})^{- \frac {1}{2}} [ (- \mathfrak {g})^{\frac {1}{2}} \Phi^{, a} ]_{, a} \right] (- \mathfrak {g})^{\frac {1}{2}}
$$
or
$$
\square \Phi - \mathrm {m}^{2} \Phi = 0. \tag {1}
$$
From Problem 21.3 the stress-energy is
$$
\begin{array}{l}
T_{\mu \nu} \equiv - 2 (- g)^{- \frac {1}{2}} \frac {\delta [ (- g)^{\frac {1}{2}} L ]}{\delta g^{\mu \nu}} \\
= \frac {1}{4 \pi} (- \mathrm {g})^{- \frac {1}{2}} \left[ \Phi_{, \mu} \Phi_{, \nu} (- \mathrm {g})^{\frac {1}{2}} - \frac {1}{2} (\Phi_{, a} \Phi^{, a} + \mathrm {m}^{2} \Phi^{2}) (- \mathrm {g})^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathrm {g}_{\mu \nu} \right] \\
= \frac {1}{4 \pi} \left[ \Phi_{, \mu} \Phi_{, \nu} - \frac {1}{2} g_{\mu \nu} \left(\Phi_{, a} \Phi^{, a} + m^{2} \Phi^{2}\right) \right] \tag {2} \\
\end{array}
$$
and hence its divergence is
$$
\mathrm {T}_{; \nu}^{\mu \nu} = \frac {1}{4 \pi} \left(\Phi_{; \nu}^{, \mu} \Phi_{; \nu}^{, \nu} + \Phi_{; \nu}^{, \mu} \square \Phi - \Phi_{, a}^{; \mu} \Phi_{, a}^{, a} - \mathrm {m}^{2} \Phi \Phi_{, a}^{, \mu}\right).
$$
For a scalar field $\Phi$ , we also have the relation
$$
\Phi_{; \nu}^{\prime \mu} = \Phi_{, \nu}^{i \mu}, \tag {4}
$$
thus Equation (3) becomes
$$
\mathrm {T}_{; \nu}^{\mu \nu} = \frac {\Phi^{\prime} \mu}{4 \pi} (\square \Phi - \mathrm {m}^{2} \Phi) \tag {5}
$$
which, by Equation (1), vanishes.
